data.title: This Healthcare Giant Just Raised Its Dividend 15%
data.description:
Healthcare concern McKesson (MCK) has raised its quarterly dividend payment by 15%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The company, which delivers pharmaceutical drugs and medical supplies, and specializes in health information technology, has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.94 per share, up 14.6% from a previous distribution of $0.82.

The new dividend gives MCK stock a yield 0.45%. The raised dividend will be paid on Oct. 1 to shareholders of record as of Sept. 1 this year. It is the 10 consecutive year that McKesson has raised its quarterly dividend as the company prioritizes returns to shareholders.

McKesson’s Continued Growth

In a news release, Brian Tyler, chair and CEO of McKesson, said the increased divided payment reflects the company’s business strength, long-term growth prospects, and its disciplined approach to capital allocation. The company operates in a niche business and specializes in medication deliveries to hospitals and other healthcare institutions.

The dividend hike comes before McKesson is next scheduled to issues its quarterly financial results on Aug. 5 of this year. In its most recent earnings report, McKesson reported earnings per share (EPS) of $11.69 and quarterly revenue of $96.30 billion. For its 2026 fiscal year, the company announced revenue of $403.4 billion and EPS of $39.11.

Is MCK Stock a Buy?

McKesson stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 12 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MCK price target of $935.55 implies 14% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Verizon (VZ) Looks Like a Fairly Attractive Buy Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Verizon (VZ) looks like a fairly attractive Buy ahead of earnings. The telecom giant, which provides wireless and broadband services across the U.S., is no longer facing the same cash-flow pressure that weighed on its stock in 2022 and 2023. Free cash flow (FCF) has recovered, the 6.6% dividend yield looks well covered, and the wireless business is finally showing signs of stabilization.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

That being said, Verizon is set to report its Q2 2026 results before the opening bell on July 24. More than a small earnings per share (EPS) beat, my bullish thesis depends on two things: another positive quarter of postpaid phone net additions and management maintaining its guidance for at least $21.5 billion in FCF.

Verizon’s Rebound Has Yet to Close the Gap

Verizon’s rebound has yet to close the gap with the broader market. The stock has recovered since hitting its October 2023 lows near $30. However, VZ traded mostly sideways throughout 2024 and 2025, continuing to underperform the market.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Conagra Brands (CAG) Cuts Dividend in Half as Financial Results Weaken
data.description:
The stock of Conagra Brands (CAG) was under pressure on July 15 after the packaged food company cut its quarterly dividend payment in half amid deteriorating financial conditions.

TipRanks Welcomes a New ETF – NYSE:RANK

  • TipRanks has entered a new arena in the investing world, powering the index of an ETF based on its unique data now trading under the ticker RANK on the NYSE.
  • RANK tracks the performance of the TipRanks US Momentum Analysts Index, a rules-based index of 50 large U.S. companies.

Conagra said that it’s cutting its annual dividend to $0.70 from $1.40 previously as it looks to conserve cash and reinvest in a turnaround at the struggling business. CAG stock previously boasted a dividend yield of 9.86%, one of the highest payouts on Wall Street.

The company has paid a distribution to shareholders for 50 consecutive years, dating back to 1976. Until now, Conagra had raised its dividend for five consecutive years, with an average annual increase of 10.50% during that time.

“Resetting our dividend to an annualized rate of $0.70 per share proactively realigns our capital allocation, accelerates progress toward our leverage target, supports critical investments, and strengthens our financial flexibility,” said CEO John Brase in the company’s earnings release.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: This Wall Street Bank Just Raised Its Dividend 11%
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) has raised its quarterly dividend by 11% following blowout financial results for this year’s second quarter.

TipRanks Welcomes a New ETF – NYSE:RANK

  • TipRanks has entered a new arena in the investing world, powering the index of an ETF based on its unique data now trading under the ticker RANK on the NYSE.
  • RANK tracks the performance of the TipRanks US Momentum Analysts Index, a rules-based index of 50 large U.S. companies.

Goldman Sachs has lifted its quarterly distribution to stockholders to $5.00 per share, up 11% from $4.50 previously. Moving forward, GS stock will have a dividend yield of nearly 2%. This is the 15th consecutive years that Goldman Sachs has increased its quarterly dividend payment.

The latest dividend increase comes after Goldman Sachs recently passed the U.S. Federal Reserve’s annual stress test that is applied to all major banks in America. Management at Goldman Sachs said they wanted to reward shareholders after the bank reported exceptionally strong financial results for this year’s second quarter.

Goldman Sachs Blows the Doors Off

Goldman Sachs announced record financial results for the April through June quarter. The bank’s profit was up 92% year-over-year. Revenue in the quarter increased 39% from a year earlier. Both quarterly revenue and earnings per share were records for the leading investment bank.

Executives at Goldman Sachs attributed the strong quarterly financial results to booming stock trading and stronger underwriting activity as the number of deals taking place on Wall Street surges. Goldman Sachs was a lead underwriter on the June initial public offering (IPO) of SpaceX (SPCX), which was the biggest market debut in history, raising $85.7 billion U.S.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

Goldman Sachs’s stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 13 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendation issued in the last three months. The average GS price target of $1,073.58 implies 2% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the bank’s financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Is Dividend King Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) a Buy Ahead of Earnings?
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is scheduled to report its fiscal second-quarter results before the market opens on July 15. JNJ stock is a “Dividend King” because it has increased its dividend for 64 consecutive years. Johnson & Johnson is one of the world’s largest and oldest healthcare companies. Currently, JNJ pays a regular quarterly dividend of $1.34 per share, reflecting an above-industry average yield of 2.5%. JNJ last increased its dividend on April 14, 2026, when the board approved a 3.1% dividend hike to $1.34 per share.

TipRanks Welcomes a New ETF – NYSE:RANK

  • TipRanks has entered a new arena in the investing world, powering the index of an ETF based on its unique data now trading under the ticker RANK on the NYSE.
  • RANK tracks the performance of the TipRanks US Momentum Analysts Index, a rules-based index of 50 large U.S. companies.

The Street expects JNJ to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $2.86, up from $2.77 reported in the prior-year period. Similarly, sales are expected to rise 5.4% year-over-year to $25.02 billion. Notably, JNJ has beaten earnings expectations in seven of the past eight quarters, while matching estimates once.

In Q1 FY26, JNJ beat earnings expectations, driven by solid performance in its Innovative Medicine segment, where drugs like Darzalex and Carvykti posted robust sales growth. The company also lifted its full-year 2026 outlook, raising the midpoint of its sales guidance to $100.8 billion and increasing adjusted EPS guidance to $11.55.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Verizon (VZ) Was Once a Safe Dividend Bet. Now It Looks Like a Value Trap
data.description:
Verizon (VZ) was once considered a safe dividend stock. Today, the traditional land-based telecom moat is cracking under a toxic mix of technological disruption and a massive financial overhang. Thus, I believe Verizon’s legendary dividend is rapidly becoming a classic value trap, and so I am bearish on the stock.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

SPCX has a long & short ETF? Explore SPCM & SPCG

For decades, buying Verizon was a no-brainer defensive play built on premium network dominance and reliable, steady cash flows. The technology and communications service provider now faces new headwinds, all while carrying a crushing debt that threatens its dividend. This is the central question hanging over income investors today as the telecom industry undergoes a massive shift.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: Levi Strauss Stock (LEVI) Gains After Strong Earnings
data.description:
The stock of Levi Strauss (LEVI) is up after the clothing retailer announced strong financial results for this year’s second quarter.

55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The company known for its blue jeans and denim shirts reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.28, which topped the consensus expectation that called for $0.24. Revenue in the period totaled $1.56 billion, which beat the $1.52 billion that had been forecast on Wall Street. Sales were up 8% from a year earlier.

Executives at the company said consumers remain resilient and are still buying clothes despite higher gas prices caused by the Iran war. Levi Strauss raised its forward guidance, saying it now expects full-year earnings per share of $1.46 to $1.52, up from $1.42 to $1.48 previously. Levi also raised its revenue outlook, saying it expects full-year sales to rise between 7% and 7.5%, up from 5.5% to 6.5% previously.

data.title: This Is Warren Buffett’s Top Dividend Stock
data.description:
Coca-Cola (NYSE: $KO) remains Warren Buffett’s best-performing dividend stock.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Buffett has famously held his current stake in KO stock since the early 1990s. He’s owned the same 400 million shares in the beverage giant for more than 30 years. And for good reason, Coca-Cola has paid Buffett billions of dollars over the years in dividend payments.

KO stock currently pays a quarterly dividend of $0.53 per share for a yield of 2.52%. However, Coke has been remarkably consistent in raising its dividend payouts over the years. The Atlanta-based company has increased its distribution to shareholders for 64 consecutive years. That makes Coca-Cola a Dividend King, defined as any company that raises its dividend for 50 years or longer.

Buffett’s Dividend Windfall

This year alone, Buffett stands to make $848 million in dividends from KO stock. The amount that the legendary investor has earned in dividends over the years has surpassed the money he spent to originally buy his stake in Coca-Cola through his Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) holding company. In 1994, after Buffett finished building his Coke stake, the company’s dividend payments to Berkshire totaled $75 million.

Buffett has consistently praised the management of Coca-Cola and the company’s commitment to rewarding shareholders through dividend payments. “Growth occurred every year, just as certain as birthdays. All Charlie and I were required to do was cash Coke’s quarterly dividend checks. We expect that those checks are highly likely to grow,” said Buffett in his 2022 letter to shareholders.

Is KO Stock a Buy?

Coca-Cola’s stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 17 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average KO price target of $88.00 implies 11.24% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

1. Mach Natural Resources (MNR)

Mach Natural Resources operates as an independent firm within the upstream oil and gas sector. data.title: DuPont (DD) Raises Its Dividend a Staggering 200%
data.description:
DuPont de Nemours (DD), a maker of construction materials, has raised its quarterly dividend payment an astounding 200% as its financial results recover after years of underperformance.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Delaware-based DuPont, which makes everything from household insulation to quartz countertops, has declared a quarterly distribution to its stockholders of $0.60 per share, a 200% increase from its previous dividend of $0.20 a share.

The new dividend gives DD stock a yield of 1.74%. The next dividend will be paid on September 15 to shareholders of record as of August 31 this year. DuPont has paid an uninterrupted quarterly dividend for more than a century, going all the way back to 1904. The company has raised its dividend each year since 2019 following the conclusion of a restructuring at DuPont.

DuPont Turns a Corner

After years of underperformance, DuPont underwent a major restructuring over the last decade designed to streamline its business operations. The company spun off its electronics business unit and its fiber business that was home to the iconic Kevlar brand. Today, DuPont focuses on technologies aimed at the healthcare sector and water purification.

The result has been that Dupont’s financial results have improved. The company most recently reported strong first quarter 2026 results, posting sales of $1.68 billion, a 4% increase year-over-year, and earnings per share (EPS) of $0.55, both of which beat Wall Street’s consensus forecasts.

In a statement, management said the improving financials have enabled DuPont to raise its dividend as much as 200%. While the divided increase of 200% is unusual, it is not unheard of for companies to raise their distribution by such big amounts. Earlier this year, Nvidia (NVDA) increased its dividend by 2,400% from $0.01 to $0.25 per share.

Is DD Stock a Buy?

DuPont’s stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average DD price target of $102.33 implies 25% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: IBM Is Turning Its Boring AI Pitch into a Real Strategy
data.description:
International Business Machines (IBM) is turning what was once viewed as a boring artificial intelligence (AI) pitch into a strategy that is starting to look increasingly valuable. The global technology and consulting company has never been the flashiest name in AI. It does not build AI models that grab headlines or chase consumer trends. Instead, IBM helps large enterprises make AI work within their existing systems. This focus is becoming increasingly relevant as businesses move from experimenting with AI to deploying it.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Explore IBX for 2X leverage on IBM

I am bullish on IBM. Software is growing fast, the AI consulting backlog is building, and free cash flow just hit its best Q1 level in a decade. For a stock trading below its own historical valuation, that combination is worth a serious look.
data.author: Hillary Remy

data.title: This Grocery Store Chain Just Raised Its Dividend 11%
data.description:
Kroger (KR), a leading U.S. grocery store chain, has raised its quarterly dividend by 11%.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Management at Kroger declared a quarterly dividend payment of $0.39 per share, an 11% increase from the previous shareholder distribution of $0.35. The new dividend is payable on Sept. 1 of this year to shareholders of record as of Aug. 15.

The new quarterly dividend gives KR stock a forward yield of 2.65%. Kroger has now raised its dividend for 20 consecutive years. The company has consistently paid a distribution to shareholders since 2006. An earlier dividend from Kroger was suspended in the early 2000s as the company underwent a restructuring.

Kroger’s New CEO

Earlier this year, Kroger named former Walmart (WMT) executive Greg Foran as its new chief executive officer (CEO). Kroger had been searching for a new CEO for more than a year after former CEO Rodney McMullen was ousted over an undisclosed personal conduct issue.

Foran was previously the CEO of Walmart’s U.S. operations. Kroger is currently the third largest grocery store chain in the U.S. as measured by sales volumes. It sits behind Walmart and Costco Wholesale (COST), which are the largest grocery store chains in America.

Kroger had tried to grow and gain market share through a proposed merger with rival grocery store chain Albertsons (ACI). However, the companies called off the proposed $25 billion merger when it faced increased regulatory scrutiny and appeared unlikely to be approved in the U.S.

Is KR Stock a Buy?
data.author: Joel Baglole
